fundamentals.name: Oklo Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Oklo Inc. develops fission power plants to provide energy at scale to customers in the United States. The company offers Aurora Powerhouse, which is designed to produce between 15 and up to 75 megawatts of electricity. It is also commercializing nuclear fuel recycling and fuel fabrication technology that can convert used nuclear fuel into usable fuel for its reactors. The company was formerly known as AltC Acquisition Corp. and changed its name to Oklo Inc. in May 2024. Oklo Inc. was founded in 2013 and is headquartered in Santa Clara, California.

judgment.summary: A growth-oriented company with substantial cash and a clean balance sheet, but currently unprofitable with no revenue.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed estimates by -65.1%; next earnings date is 2026-08-10.
positives: Substantial cash balance of $2.21B supports operations and growth initiatives.
Low Debt/Equity ratio of 0.10 indicates a healthy balance sheet.
Strong analyst consensus with a 'buy' rating and a mean target of $88.74.
High current and quick ratios (59.93 and 59.73) suggest excellent short-term liquidity.
negatives: Zero revenue and margins (Gross, Operating, Profit) indicate the business is not yet commercialized.
Negative Return on Equity (-8.9%) and Return on Assets (-7.2%) show poor profitability.
Negative Free Cash Flow of $-100.00M indicates cash burn.
Forward P/E of -84.08 reflects the current lack of earnings.

news.paragraph: Oklo's stock surged Tuesday following news that the nuclear energy startup is moving closer to commercialization. A key development includes a win from the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) and a deal with Meta, which reframes Oklo's ambitions for powering AI data centers. These catalysts suggest a significant step forward for the company's operational and strategic goals.

headlines.title: Nuclear Energy Startup Oklo's Stock Is Surging Tuesday—Here's Why
Oklo Moves One Step Closer to Commercialization
Oklo stock is surging — here's why
Oklo’s NRC Win And Meta Deal Reframe Its AI Power Ambitions
